assertion.text:
Erdős Problem #1131: declared status 'open'. Formalized: no. For $x_1,\ldots,x_n\in [-1,1]$ let\[l_k(x)= rac{\prod_{i
eq k}(x-x_i)}{\prod_{i
eq k}(x_k-x_i)},\]which are such that $l_k(x_k)=1$ and $l_k(x_i)=0$ for $i
eq k$.

What is the minimal value of\[I(x_1,\ldots,x_n)=\int_{-1}^1 \sum_k \lvert l_k(x)vert^2\mathrm{d}x?\]In particular, is it true that\[\min I =2-(1+o(1)) rac{1}{n}?\] Current best: Erd\H{o}s, Szabados, Varma, and V'{e}rtesi \cite{ESVV94} proved that\[2-O\left( rac{(\log n)^2}{n}ight)\leq \min I\leq 2- rac{2}{2n-1}\]where the upper bound is witnessed by the roots of the integral of the Legendre polynomial as above. Prize: no. Tags: analysis, polynomials.
